name: Hexokinase reaction
deltaGNote: Strongly exergonic and physiologically irreversible; committed first step of glucose trapping in the cell.
mechanismNotes: Transfers the gamma-phosphate of ATP to the 6-hydroxyl of glucose. Requires Mg2+-ATP as the true substrate.

name: Phosphoglucose isomerase reaction
mechanismNotes: Aldose-ketose isomerization via a cis-enediol intermediate.

name: Phosphofructokinase-1 reaction
deltaGNote: Strongly exergonic and physiologically irreversible; the committed, allosterically regulated step of glycolysis proper.
mechanismNotes: Transfers the gamma-phosphate of ATP to the 1-hydroxyl of F6P. Rate-limiting and heavily regulated by AMP/ATP/citrate (F2,6BP-driven hormonal control deferred, see docs/schema.md).

name: Aldolase reaction
deltaGNote: Endergonic under standard conditions but pulled forward physiologically by rapid downstream consumption of triose phosphates.
mechanismNotes: Aldol cleavage of F1,6BP into two 3-carbon triose phosphates (class I aldolase, Schiff-base mechanism in humans).

name: Triose phosphate isomerase reaction
deltaGNote: Near-equilibrium; strongly favors DHAP at equilibrium but G3P is continuously drained downstream, pulling flux forward.
mechanismNotes: Interconverts the two triose phosphates via a cis-enediol intermediate, effectively routing both aldolase products through the rest of glycolysis as G3P.

name: Glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase reaction
deltaGNote: Near-equilibrium; the only redox (NAD+/NADH) step in glycolysis proper.
mechanismNotes: Oxidizes the aldehyde of G3P and couples the resulting acyl-enzyme intermediate to inorganic phosphate, producing a high-energy acyl-phosphate.

name: Phosphoglycerate kinase reaction
deltaGNote: Near-equilibrium; first substrate-level phosphorylation step of glycolysis.
mechanismNotes: Transfers the high-energy acyl-phosphate of 1,3-BPG to ADP.

name: Phosphoglycerate mutase reaction
mechanismNotes: Intramolecular phosphoryl shift from C3 to C2 via a 2,3-bisphosphoglycerate enzyme intermediate.

name: Enolase reaction
deltaGNote: Near-equilibrium dehydration; concentrates chemical potential into a high-energy enol phosphate.
mechanismNotes: Mg2+-dependent dehydration of 2-PG to form the enol phosphate PEP.

name: Pyruvate kinase reaction
deltaGNote: Strongly exergonic and physiologically irreversible; second substrate-level phosphorylation and final committed step of glycolysis.
mechanismNotes: Transfers the phosphate of PEP to ADP; spontaneous tautomerization of the resulting enolpyruvate to pyruvate contributes most of the driving force.
